blockers.why: RAPP/1 section 13 makes the authenticated, monotonic, freshness-checked registry the estate root of trust. No owner-provided trust anchor or verifiable registry is present.
blockers.what: The estate owner must publish and authenticate the section 13 registry. Repository maintainers must not invent any missing trust material.
blockers.how: Provide the out-of-band estate_owner rappid and SPKI, then publish a section 13 document whose detached signature, sequence, entries, source, and freshness evidence satisfy the pinned rev-5 specification.

acceptance_tests.assertion: The estate_owner rappid is supplied out of band and its tail equals Hb("rapp/1:rappid", SPKI_DER).
The publication is an I-JSON document with schema exactly "rapp/1-registry", a uint53 registry_seq, the required append-only entries, and a detached signature.
The detached signature verifies over canonical(registry without sig), and its kid is the supplied estate_owner rappid.
registry_seq is not lower than the highest registry_seq previously verified by the consumer.
The registry is obtained from canonical_source or an authenticated provenance-stamped mirror and is within the owner-defined staleness policy.
Every protocol, kind, egg-variant, error-code, genesis, SPKI, tombstone, re-anchor, estate-owner, and master-plan entry present is validated against section 13.3 before use.
Tests refuse an absent anchor, absent or invalid signature, SPKI-tail mismatch, rollback sequence, stale evidence, unknown namespace value, and malformed entry.

prohibited_substitutes: Byte equality or structural hash equality without owner authentication
Locally invented owner keys, signatures, sequence values, anchors, re-anchors, tombstones, genesis entries, or acceptance state
